amount.note: The non-qualified deferred compensation plan is described as unfunded and represents a general unsecured deferred obligation to executives. However, the amounts disclosed cover only the three U.S. NEOs (an executive-compensation table), not the full company plan liability, and no plan-wide obligation figure is provided. Because this is a partial NEO-only disclosure and not a complete off-balance-sheet obligation figure, it is not usable in a company-level economic-debt bridge; informational only.

baskets.name: Loans to officers/employees basket (Investment)
baskets.value: $10.0 million aggregate outstanding at any time
baskets.quote: (3) not exceeding $10.0 million in the aggregate outstanding at any time.

covenants.unrestricted_subsidiary_designation_flex: The indenture contemplates designation of Unrestricted Subsidiaries: the Investment definition addresses the mechanics of designating a Restricted Subsidiary as an Unrestricted Subsidiary (capturing the proportionate fair market value of net assets as an "Investment") and re-designation back to Restricted. A dedicated Section 4.15 "Specified IP Covenant" and the "Specified IP License Agreements" defined term operate as a J. Crew-style constraint on movement of material IP. Full designation conditions are not reproduced in the provided text.
covenants.anchor_clause: "solely with respect to Aristech or Altuglas: (a) Investments in the form of deposits made to Trinseo Ireland Global IHB Limited using cash generated by Aristech and Altuglas, as applicable, in the ordinary course of business, which loans shall be made in the ordinary course and consistent with past practice; provided, that neither Aristech nor Altuglas shall make any additional investments pursuant to this clause (a) following the occurrence and during the continuance of (i) an Event of Default pursuant to clause (8) or (9)…"

lme_vectors.rationale: A dedicated Specified IP Covenant (Section 4.15) plus the Specified IP License Agreements construct restrict movement of material IP, and the Investment definition disciplines the fair-market-value leakage on unrestricted-subsidiary designation.
lme_vectors.basis: Section 4.15 'Specified IP Covenant'; the 'Specified IP License Agreements' defined term (Altuglas and Aristech amended and restated IP license agreements dated January 17, 2025); Investment definition treating designation of an Unrestricted Subsidiary as a permanent Investment measured at fair market value.
lme_vectors.quote: Section 4.15. Specified IP Covenant.

lme_vectors.rationale: Section 4.6 governs Restricted Payments and the RP definition is broad (capturing dividends, redemptions, returns of capital and Restricted Investments), but the operative RP builder/basket thresholds are not reproduced in the provided text so capacity cannot be sized.
lme_vectors.basis: Section 4.6 Restricted Payments; definition of 'Restricted Payment' including any dividend/distribution on Equity Interests and any Restricted Investment; 'Restricted Investment' means any Investment other than a Permitted Investment.
lme_vectors.quote: means (i) any dividend or other distribution (whether in cash, securities or other property) with respect to any Equity Interest of any Note Party… and (ii) any Restricted Investment.

lme_vectors.rationale: Section 4.2 governs Indebtedness and defined terms (Credit Facility, Refinancing Indebtedness, Incur) permit refinancing and increases, but the specific incremental/ratio debt permissions and caps are not contained in the provided text, so capacity cannot be assessed.
lme_vectors.basis: Section 4.2 Indebtedness; 'Credit Facility' definition expressly including agreements '(3) increasing the amount of Indebtedness Incurred thereunder'; Refinancing Indebtedness definition permitting refunding plus premiums, fees and defeasance costs.
lme_vectors.quote: the term "Credit Facility" shall include any agreement or instrument… (3) increasing the amount of Indebtedness Incurred thereunder or available to be borrowed thereunder or (4) otherwise altering the terms and conditions thereof.

lme_vectors.rationale: The indenture contains an anti-serial-vote protection: Additional Notes incurred substantially concurrently with a Liability Management Transaction to influence a consent are excluded from the Required Holders count for that consent, limiting sponsor stacking of votes for an uptier.
lme_vectors.basis: Definitional provision requiring an Officer's Certificate at incurrence of Additional Notes tied to a Liability Management Transaction and excluding such holders from Required Holders determinations for the concurrent consent.
lme_vectors.quote: the holders of such Additional Notes shall not be included in the determination of Required Holders for any consent or approval that is being sought substantially concurrently with the incurrence of such Additional Notes.

citation.quote: "solely with respect to Aristech or Altuglas: (a) Investments in the form of deposits made to Trinseo Ireland Global IHB Limited using cash generated by Aristech and Altuglas, as applicable, in the ordinary course of business, which loans shall be made in the ordinary course and consistent with past practice; provided, that neither Aristech nor Altuglas shall make any additional investments pursuant to this clause (a) following the occurrence and during the continuance of (i) an Event of Default pursuant to clause (8) or (9)…"

baskets.quote: on the Closing Date, in a single borrowing in an aggregate principal amount requested by the Lead Borrower not to exceed $35,000,000

baskets.name: Permitted Investments (enumerated categories (a)-(hh))
baskets.value: Enumerated categories only (no general dollar-cap builder disclosed in excerpt); includes existing Investments, intercompany subordinated Investments, JV Interests ownership, cash/Cash Equivalents/Investment Grade Securities, deposits by Aristech/Altuglas to Trinseo Ireland Global IHB Limited, securitization Investments, etc.
baskets.quote: the Lead Borrower, in its sole discretion, will classify and may subsequently reclassify such Investment (or any portion thereof) in any one or more of the types of Investments described in clauses (a) through (hh) above

covenants.unrestricted_subsidiary_designation_flex: Highly constrained. Under Section 5.14, the Co-Borrower, Holdings, Altuglas and Aristech "are and shall remain an 'Unrestricted Subsidiary' of the Trinseo Lead Borrower and its Restricted Subsidiaries for purposes of... the Prepetition OpCo Credit Agreement, the Prepetition Super-Priority Revolving Credit Agreement and any other Indebtedness... at all times thereafter." This is a fixed designation within a DIP structure rather than a flexible re-designation mechanic; the excerpt does not disclose a general basket permitting new unrestricted-subsidiary designations by the Loan Parties.
covenants.anchor_clause: Section 7.14 Specified IP Covenant, read together with Section 5.14 Designation and Section 5.15 Intellectual Property. Section 5.14 provides: "As of the Closing Date and at all times thereafter, each of the Co-Borrower, Holdings, Altuglas and Aristech are and shall remain an 'Unrestricted Subsidiary' of the Trinseo Lead Borrower and its Restricted Subsidiaries for purposes of, in accordance with and pursuant to the Prepetition OpCo Credit Agreement, the Prepetition Super-Priority Revolving Credit Agreement and any other Indebtedness of the Trinseo Lead Borrower and its Restricted Subsidiaries at any time outstanding, including without limitation, any refinancing Indebtedness of any of the foregoing." Section 5.15: "Trinseo Europe owns or otherwise has the right to license to Aristech and Altuglas, as applicable, the Specified IP Rights licensed under the applicable Specified IP License Agreement... Other than Trinseo Europe, Aristech and Altuglas, no other Loan Party owns, licenses or possesses the right to use any Specified IP Rights in North America."

lme_vectors.rationale: The agreement contains a dedicated Specified IP Covenant (Section 7.14) and represents that IP ownership/licensing is confined to identified entities (Trinseo Europe, Aristech, Altuglas), constraining transfer of material IP away from the credit group.
lme_vectors.basis: Section 7.14 Specified IP Covenant (TOC) plus Section 5.15 representation that only Trinseo Europe, Aristech and Altuglas own/license Specified IP Rights in North America, with a defined Specified IP License Agreement.
lme_vectors.quote: Other than Trinseo Europe, Aristech and Altuglas, no other Loan Party owns, licenses or possesses the right to use any Specified IP Rights in North America.

lme_vectors.rationale: New money is sized as fixed New Money Commitments (Initial $35,000,000 plus Delayed Draw up to unfunded commitments) with no reborrowing, but the operative Section 7.03 Indebtedness permitted-debt capacities are not contained in the provided excerpt.
lme_vectors.basis: Section 2.01 caps the Initial New Money Term Loans at $35,000,000 and Delayed Draw loans at unfunded New Money Commitments; Section 7.03 Indebtedness exists but its baskets are not in the excerpt.
lme_vectors.quote: In no event shall any Lender be required to make Term Loans in excess of its New Money Commitments. Once repaid or prepaid, the New Money Term Loans may not be reborrowed.

lme_vectors.rationale: This is a super-priority DIP facility with a Required Lenders construct that specially protects Designated Lenders holding >=75% of New Money, but the sacred-rights/voting amendment provisions governing priming are not in the provided text.
lme_vectors.basis: Required Lenders definition includes a Designated Lender protection and disregards certain Roll-Up Term Loans; Roll-Up occurs only with Required Lenders' consent.
lme_vectors.quote: Required Lenders shall include each Designated Lender for so long as such Designated Lender (together with its managed funds and accounts) holds at least 75% of the New Money Commitments and New Money Term Loans

lme_vectors.rationale: Section 7.06 Restricted Payments exists and Restricted Payments are broadly defined to include Restricted Investments, but the specific RP baskets and thresholds are not contained in the provided excerpt.
lme_vectors.basis: Section 7.06 Restricted Payments (TOC); Restricted Payment definition captures dividends, redemptions, returns of capital and any Restricted Investment; Permitted Investments are enumerated in clauses (a)-(hh).
lme_vectors.quote: any dividend or other distribution (whether in cash, securities or other property) with respect to any Equity Interest of any Loan Party... and (ii) any Restricted Investment.

citation.quote: Section 7.14 Specified IP Covenant, read together with Section 5.14 Designation and Section 5.15 Intellectual Property. Section 5.14 provides: "As of the Closing Date and at all times thereafter, each of the Co-Borrower, Holdings, Altuglas and Aristech are and shall remain an 'Unrestricted Subsidiary' of the Trinseo Lead Borrower and its Restricted Subsidiaries for purposes of, in accordance with and pursuant to the Prepetition OpCo Credit Agreement, the Prepetition Super-Priority Revolving Credit Agreement and any other Indebtedness of the Trinseo Lead Borrower and its Restricted Subsidiaries at any time outstanding, including without limitation, any refinancing Indebtedness of any of the foregoing." Section 5.15: "Trinseo Europe owns or otherwise has the right to license to Aristech and Altuglas, as applicable, the Specified IP Rights licensed under the applicable Specified IP License Agreement... Other than Trinseo Europe, Aristech and Altuglas, no other Loan Party owns, licenses or possesses the right to use any Specified IP Rights in North America."

covenants.unrestricted_subsidiary_designation_flex: Not addressed in the provided text. This is a bankruptcy-remote receivables securitization SPV structure (DIP-context amendment and restatement) rather than a corporate credit facility with restricted/unrestricted subsidiary architecture; no unrestricted subsidiary designation mechanic appears.
covenants.anchor_clause: "Carve Out" has the meaning given to such term in the Eligible DIP Order; provided that any amendment, modification or supplement to the Carve Out after the date of entry of the Eligible DIP Order that would be materially adverse to the interests of the Administrative Agent, the Collateral Agent or any Lender (each in such capacity) shall not be effective for purposes of this Agreement without the prior written consent (not to be unreasonably withheld) of the affected Administrative Agent or Lender, as applicable.

lme_vectors.rationale: This is a receivables securitization operating in a Chapter 11 / DIP context where superpriority claim ranking is governed by DIP orders, and the text conditions Eligible DIP Facility on DIP Superpriority Claims not ranking senior to or pari passu with the AR Superpriority Claims, but no lender-vote/sacred-rights priming mechanic is shown.
lme_vectors.basis: Eligible DIP Facility definition requires that no DIP Superpriority Claims rank senior to or pari passu with the AR Superpriority Claims; Carve Out defined by reference to the Eligible DIP Order.
lme_vectors.quote: no DIP Superpriority Claims shall rank senior to or pari passu with the AR Superpriority Claims

lme_vectors.rationale: The Eligible DIP Facility criteria prohibit any Adverse Claim on the Purchased Receivables, Collections, Collection Accounts or other AR Facility Collateral, protecting the collateral pool from being transferred or subordinated outside the facility.
lme_vectors.basis: Eligible DIP Facility definition: not secured by any Adverse Claim on equity of Borrower/U.S. Intermediate Transferor, any Purchased Receivables, Related Security or Collections, the Collection Accounts or any other AR Facility Collateral.
lme_vectors.quote: such DIP Facility is not secured by any Adverse Claim on (i) the equity interests of the Borrower or the U.S. Intermediate Transferor, (ii) any Purchased Receivables, Related Security or Collections, (iii) the Collection Accounts or any other Transaction Account or any funds on deposit therein, or (iv) any other AR Facility Collateral

lme_vectors.rationale: The text contemplates competing receivables facilities and DIP facilities but constrains them (Competing Transaction restrictions and Eligible DIP Facility criteria) rather than granting a defined incremental debt capacity; the actual incremental/negative-covenant debt provisions are not in the excerpt.
lme_vectors.basis: Competing Transaction definition captures any asset-based/borrowing base/factoring facility involving sale or pledge of receivables, with only a limited ordinary-course factoring carve-out capped at EUR4,500,000 per Calculation Period.
lme_vectors.quote: factoring facilities entered into in the ordinary course of business involving the sale of Receivables for which the Obligor is Coexpan S.A., Torraspapel, S.A. or their respective Subsidiaries, in an amount not to exceed €4,500,000 in the aggregate in any Calculation Period shall not be considered a Competing Transaction

covenants.unrestricted_subsidiary_designation_flex: Severely constrained. No Loan Party may form or acquire any direct Subsidiary after the Closing Date other than the Specified Subsidiary (a newly-formed wholly-owned Subsidiary of the Co-Borrower that may hold only the JV Interests and may not incur Indebtedness or suffer Liens, with failure being an Event of Default). Section 7.02: "No Loan Party shall form or acquire any direct Subsidiary after the Closing Date, other than the Specified Subsidiary."
covenants.anchor_clause: "(a)the JV Sale so long as (i) prior to the Specified Contribution, the Co-Borrower and following the Specified Contribution, the Specified SPV, receives at least Fair Market Value therefor, (ii) 75% of the consideration is paid in cash or Cash Equivalents (in each case free and clear of all Liens at the time received), (iii) at the date of entry into a definitive document for the JV Sale, no Default or Event of Default exists or will result therefrom, and on the closing date of the JV Sale, no Event of Default pursuant to Section 8.01(a), (f) or (g) exists or will result therefrom; and (iv) proceeds of such Disposition are applied to prepay the Term Loans in accordance with Section 2.05(b)(ii) of this Agreement;"

lme_vectors.rationale: The Specified Subsidiary that receives the JV Interests via the Specified Contribution is expressly caged — it may hold no other assets, may not incur debt or liens, and its formation is the only permitted new subsidiary, closing off asset-stripping into an unrestricted vehicle.

financial_covenants.kind: First Lien Net Leverage Ratio — springing mandatory prepayment trigger (post-JV Sale)

financial_covenants.springing_trigger: Following the JV Sale, if First Lien Net Leverage Ratio exceeds 3.75:1.00 as of the most recently ended Test Period AND the Lead Borrower receives any Excess Interest or Excess Amortization
financial_covenants.quote: Following the JV Sale, if (A) the First Lien Net Leverage Ratio, calculated on a pro forma basis in accordance with Section 1.10 of the Trinseo Credit Agreement, exceeds 3.503.75:1.00 as of the most recently ended Test Period ... following the JV Sale and (B) the Lead Borrower receives any Excess Interest or Excess Amortization at any time following the JV Sale, the Borrowers shall cause to be prepaid ... an aggregate principal amount of Term Loans in an amount equal to 100% of Excess Interest and Excess Amortization

starter.note: (i) any dividend or other distribution (whether in cash, securities or other property) with respect to any Equity Interest of any Loan Party ... and (ii) any Restricted Investment.

capacity.formula: starter $0M + Σ quarterly [0.5×NI if NI>0 else 1.0×NI + equity issuance − dividends − buybacks] over Q3 2023→Q1 2026 ($-1,318M), floored at 0
capacity.note: omits junior-debt-issuance proceeds and unrestricted-subsidiary investment returns (not deterministically extractable); cumulation anchors at the lookback-window start — a proxy for the issuance-date build convention; builder negative — cumulative deductions exceed credits, $0 capacity

rp_basket.formula_note: equity_issuance_proceeds, stock_repurchases not tagged in XBRL — treated as $0
rp_basket.covenant_value: Not quantified in provided text; Restricted Payment defined to include dividends/distributions and Restricted Investments
rp_basket.covenant_quote: (i) any dividend or other distribution (whether in cash, securities or other property) with respect to any Equity Interest of any Loan Party ... and (ii) any Restricted Investment.
notes: issuer RP basket extracted as a stub ('Not quantified in provided text; Restricted Payment defined to include dividends') — builder-only starter $0

rows.name: Roll-Up Term Loans (roll up of Prepetition Super HoldCo Secured Obligations)
rows.value: Roll-up of prepetition Super HoldCo Secured Obligations per DIP Orders; roll only with Required Lenders' consent
rows.quote: shall mean the 'roll up' of Prepetition Super HoldCo Secured Obligations into Roll-Up Term Loans pursuant to the terms of this Agreement and the DIP Orders
rows.detail: lien covenant present — capacity not quantified in the extraction window

uptier_priming.rationale: The indenture contains an anti-serial-vote protection: Additional Notes incurred substantially concurrently with a Liability Management Transaction to influence a consent are excluded from the Required Holders count for that consent, limiting sponsor stacking of votes for an uptier.

liens_headroom.derivation: deterministic $ / % / ratio tokens over cached covenant extractions; NTA headroom = pct × (total assets − intangibles incl. goodwill) from the asset snapshot; absolute-$ baskets are stated capacity, never headroom (Moyer ch. 9)

warnings: Maturity unknown for 5 instrument(s) (2028 Refinance Term Loans, 2028 Term Loan B, Super-Priority Revolver, Accounts Receivable Securitization Facility, Other Indebtedness) — they are missing from the maturity wall.
